items.title: AI agents can edit Office files
items.oneliner: Single binary lets agents read, render, and edit Word, Excel, and PowerPoint without Microsoft Office installed.

items.title: Browser embeddings without a server
items.oneliner: 7 MB WASM embedding model runs fully in browser and returns text embeddings in about 5 ms on CPU.

items.title: A reMarkable AI diary hack
items.oneliner: Turns a reMarkable into a handwritten chat interface where ink fades and AI replies appear directly on e-ink.

items.title: Open source SaaS replacement directory
items.oneliner: Searchable directory helps teams swap paid SaaS tools for open-source alternatives they can self-host today.

items.title: OpenSSH gets security fixes
items.oneliner: Latest OpenSSH release adds hardening, stricter protocol handling, and sandbox fixes worth rolling into production images.

items.title: A practical RAG pruning walkthrough
items.oneliner: Explains how a small model prunes 68% of RAG context while keeping 96% recall in production assistants.

items.title: A playable neural Rocket League
items.oneliner: Open model generates a live playable Rocket League world from video, showing real-time world modeling in practice.

items.title: A huge model with GGUF support
items.oneliner: 432B model ships with day-one GGUF support, making immediate local experimentation easier for quantization-heavy workflows.

items.title: A fast Replit app tutorial
items.oneliner: Step-by-step guide shows how to turn a simple mobile app idea into a working prototype in minutes.

items.title: Linux on an Atari Jaguar
items.oneliner: Deep technical walkthrough shows what it takes to port Linux to a 1993 console with no MMU.
